taxonomy: Dentillaria lineata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Dentillaria, which is a small group of orchids. Dentillaria lineata is further classified into the tribe Orchideae, a subfamily of orchids that contains over 10,000 species. The classification of Dentillaria lineata is: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Angiospermophyta, Class: Liliopsida, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Genus: Dentillaria, Species: D. lineata. uses: Dentillaria lineata, also known as the striped coralroot, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its roots are said to be used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. However, there is limited scientific research on the effectiveness of Dentillaria lineata as a medicinal herb.
